rawText:
The rules governing how companies are run, how employees are managed, and how shareholders exert their influence are being rewritten simultaneously on multiple fronts -- from the industrialisation of activist investing to the disruption of entrenched industries by technology, the defence of mundane work against automation, the geopolitical buffeting of corporate turnarounds, and the surprisingly tricky science of simply asking workers what they think.

Elliott Management, the hedge fund that has industrialised what was once the artisanal business of telling bosses they are wrong, now pursues as many public activism campaigns as its four largest rivals combined, deploying legions of investment and research staff to demand everything from board replacements at Norwegian Cruise Line to the break-up of Honeywell -- a force so dominant that one banker observes, "There's Elliott and then there's everyone else."

The irony embedded in Elliott's dominance is that its demands -- returning capital to shareholders, selling off assets, simplifying unwieldy conglomerates -- have been the reigning orthodoxy of boardrooms for decades, yet the rise of giant passive investment firms like BlackRock and Vanguard, which hold vast positions but rarely intervene, has created a vacuum that necessitates an equally massive activist to stand up for the interests of those who actually own the companies.

While activists prod corporations from the outside, a new wave of disrupters is assaulting American health care from within, as venture-capital funding for digital-health startups surged to $14 billion in 2025 -- fuelled by soaring insurance premiums that have risen 50% since 2015, plummeting trust in doctors, and advances in generative AI that now allow more than a quarter of ChatGPT's 900 million weekly users to ask medical questions that once required an expensive office visit.

The health-care insurgency illustrates a broader corporate truth: that industries which resist disruption for years -- whether through regulatory capture, complexity, or sheer opacity -- often face the most violent transformation once the dam breaks, as patients who once deferred to a paternalistic medical hierarchy now demand the same consumer agency they exercise in every other sphere of their lives.

At the opposite end of the corporate spectrum from high-tech disruption sits the humble question of grunt work, which -- contrary to the breathless promises of AI evangelists -- serves functions far more valuable than its tedium suggests, from filling the days of junior employees who are learning the basics of office life to providing the cognitive downtime that, according to research from the University of Texas, allows creative ideas to incubate and minds to wander productively.

BASF, the German chemicals giant that lost its crown as the world's largest by revenue after Russia's invasion of Ukraine severed its access to cheap gas, exemplifies how even the most disciplined corporate turnaround -- involving 4 billion euros in cost cuts, the sale of a coatings division, and plans to list an agricultural unit -- can be thrown into uncertainty by geopolitical shocks, as the Iran war now threatens to destroy demand and trap a quarter of the world's ethylene and ammonia supply behind the Strait of Hormuz.

The ritual of the annual employee survey, a staple of modern management that companies deploy to gauge worker sentiment, conceals its own paradox: organisations that genuinely care about what their employees think often have the least need for formal surveys, while those that rely on them most heavily tend to be the places where workers, confronted with a Likert scale and the proposition that their bosses communicate like bananas, prudently choose "Neither Agree Nor Disagree."

Artificial intelligence is reshaping not just how companies compete but how they understand themselves, as algorithms now parse unstructured employee reviews on sites like GlassDoor to identify high-pressure cultures predictive of future corporate scandals -- a development that, combined with the AI-driven disruption of industries from health care to chemicals, suggests that the technology's deepest impact on corporate life may lie less in automating tasks than in making the previously invisible dynamics of organisations legible for the first time.

What unites these disparate threads -- the activist fund demanding simplification, the startup promising patients agency, the researcher defending drudgery, the chemical executive navigating war, the survey designer wrestling with honesty -- is the recognition that corporate life is governed not by timeless laws but by norms that shift with technology, geopolitics, and the evolving expectations of the people who work within them, a reality that makes adaptability, rather than any single strategy, the most valuable corporate asset of all.

overview.title: The Changing Rules of Corporate Life

sentences.original: The rules governing how companies are run, how employees are managed, and how shareholders exert their influence are being rewritten simultaneously on multiple fronts -- from the industrialisation of activist investing to the disruption of entrenched industries by technology, the defence of mundane work against automation, the geopolitical buffeting of corporate turnarounds, and the surprisingly tricky science of simply asking workers what they think.
sentences.translation: 기업이 어떻게 운영되고, 직원이 어떻게 관리되며, 주주가 어떻게 영향력을 행사하는지를 규정하는 규칙이 여러 전선에서 동시에 다시 쓰이고 있다 — 행동주의 투자의 산업화에서부터 기술에 의한 기득권 산업의 파괴, 자동화에 맞선 단순 업무의 수호, 기업 턴어라운드에 대한 지정학적 타격, 그리고 직원에게 의견을 묻는 일의 놀라울 만큼 까다로운 과학에 이르기까지.

grammarElements.text: The rules governing how companies are run, how employees are managed, and how shareholders exert their influence
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 현재분사 수식 + 3중 how절

grammarElements.text: are being rewritten
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 수동태

grammarElements.text: simultaneously on multiple fronts
grammarElements.label: 부사구

grammarElements.text: from the industrialisation ... to the ... science of simply asking workers what they think
grammarElements.label: from A to B 열거구

vocabulary.example: Entrenched industries often resist change until forced to adapt.
The company endured years of geopolitical buffeting before stabilising.

expressions.usage: 동시에 여러 방향에서 변화나 도전이 일어남을 군사 비유로 표현

patterns.pattern: The rules governing [X] are being rewritten on multiple fronts -- from [A] to [B], [C], [D], and [E]
patterns.explanation: 변화의 범위를 열거형으로 전개하여 글 전체의 주제를 예고하는 패턴
examples: The rules governing global trade are being rewritten on multiple fronts -- from tariffs to supply chains, currency wars, and digital regulation.
The rules governing education are being rewritten -- from classroom design to assessment, teacher training, and student wellbeing.

drillQuestions.question: The rules are being rewritten simultaneously on multiple ______.

sentences.notes: from A to B 열거 구조가 글의 다섯 가지 주제를 한 문장에 미리 보여주는 서론 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The rules governing how companies are run
are being rewritten simultaneously on multiple fronts
koreanAlignment.ko: 여러 전선에서 동시에 다시 쓰이고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the industrialisation of activist investing
the surprisingly tricky science of simply asking workers what they think
koreanAlignment.ko: 직원에게 의견을 묻는 일의 놀라울 만큼 까다로운 과학

pronunciationNotes: industrialisation: /ɪnˌdʌstriəlaɪˈzeɪʃən/ 다섯 번째 음절에 주 강세

sentences.original: Elliott Management, the hedge fund that has industrialised what was once the artisanal business of telling bosses they are wrong, now pursues as many public activism campaigns as its four largest rivals combined, deploying legions of investment and research staff to demand everything from board replacements at Norwegian Cruise Line to the break-up of Honeywell -- a force so dominant that one banker observes, "There's Elliott and then there's everyone else."
sentences.translation: 엘리엇 매니지먼트는 한때 경영자에게 잘못을 지적하는 장인적 사업이었던 것을 산업화한 헤지펀드로서, 이제 가장 큰 경쟁사 네 곳을 합친 것만큼 많은 공개 행동주의 캠페인을 수행하며 다수의 투자·리서치 인력을 동원하여 노르웨이안 크루즈 라인의 이사회 교체에서 하니웰 분할에 이르기까지 모든 것을 요구한다 — 한 은행가가 "엘리엇이 있고, 그 다음에 나머지가 있다"고 말할 만큼 압도적인 세력이다.

grammarElements.text: Elliott Management, the hedge fund that has industrialised ...
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: now pursues
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: as many public activism campaigns as its four largest rivals combined
grammarElements.label: as many A as B 비교 목적어

grammarElements.text: deploying legions of ... staff to demand everything from ... to ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (수단)

vocabulary.example: What began as an artisanal craft has become a factory-scale operation.

expressions.phrase: There's X and then there's everyone else
expressions.meaning: X가 있고, 나머지는 비교도 안 된다

patterns.pattern: [Entity] has industrialised what was once the artisanal business of [activity]
patterns.explanation: 소규모 활동이 대규모 체계적 운영으로 변했음을 표현하는 패턴
examples: Amazon has industrialised what was once the artisanal business of bookselling.
Spotify has industrialised what was once the artisanal business of music curation.

drillQuestions.question: Elliott has industrialised what was once the ______ business of telling bosses they are wrong.

sentences.notes: artisanal과 industrialised의 대비가 행동주의 투자의 규모 변화를 선명하게 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: the hedge fund that has industrialised what was once the artisanal business
koreanAlignment.ko: 한때 장인적 사업이었던 것을 산업화한 헤지펀드

koreanAlignment.en: as many public activism campaigns as its four largest rivals combined
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 큰 경쟁사 네 곳을 합친 것만큼 많은 공개 행동주의 캠페인

koreanAlignment.en: a force so dominant

sentences.original: The irony embedded in Elliott's dominance is that its demands -- returning capital to shareholders, selling off assets, simplifying unwieldy conglomerates -- have been the reigning orthodoxy of boardrooms for decades, yet the rise of giant passive investment firms like BlackRock and Vanguard, which hold vast positions but rarely intervene, has created a vacuum that necessitates an equally massive activist to stand up for the interests of those who actually own the companies.
sentences.translation: 엘리엇의 지배력에 내재된 역설은, 주주에게 자본을 돌려주고 자산을 매각하며 비대한 복합기업을 단순화하라는 그들의 요구가 수십 년간 이사회의 지배적 정통이었다는 것이다. 그런데도 블랙록이나 뱅가드처럼 막대한 지분을 보유하지만 거의 개입하지 않는 거대 패시브 투자 회사의 부상이 진공 상태를 만들었고, 실제 회사를 소유한 이들의 이익을 대변하기 위해 똑같이 거대한 행동주의 투자자가 필요하게 되었다.

grammarElements.text: The irony embedded in Elliott's dominance
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: be동사

grammarElements.text: that its demands have been the reigning orthodoxy
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 보어

grammarElements.text: yet the rise of giant passive investment firms ... has created a vacuum
grammarElements.label: yet 대조절

vocabulary.example: Shareholder primacy has been the reigning orthodoxy in corporate governance.

expressions.usage: 기존 역할을 수행하던 주체가 사라져 공백이 생겼음을 표현

patterns.pattern: The irony embedded in [situation] is that [expected norm], yet [unexpected consequence]
patterns.explanation: 지배적 규범이 있음에도 불구하고 역설적 결과가 나타남을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The irony embedded in free-market ideology is that it demands regulation to function, yet its advocates resist oversight.
The irony embedded in efficiency gains is that they create wealth, yet that wealth often concentrates at the top.

drillQuestions.question: The rise of giant passive investment firms has created a ______ that necessitates an equally massive activist.

sentences.notes: yet 절이 역설의 핵심을 전달한다: 정통 자체가 기능 부전에 빠져 행동주의자가 필요해졌다.

koreanAlignment.en: The irony embedded in Elliott's dominance
the reigning orthodoxy of boardrooms
has created a vacuum
those who actually own the companies

sentences.original: While activists prod corporations from the outside, a new wave of disrupters is assaulting American health care from within, as venture-capital funding for digital-health startups surged to $14 billion in 2025 -- fuelled by soaring insurance premiums that have risen 50% since 2015, plummeting trust in doctors, and advances in generative AI that now allow more than a quarter of ChatGPT's 900 million weekly users to ask medical questions that once required an expensive office visit.
sentences.translation: 행동주의 투자자들이 외부에서 기업을 밀어붙이는 동안, 새로운 파괴자들의 물결이 내부에서 미국 의료를 공격하고 있다. 디지털 헬스 스타트업에 대한 벤처캐피털 투자가 2025년에 140억 달러로 급증했으며 — 2015년 이후 50% 오른 보험료, 의사에 대한 급락하는 신뢰, 그리고 챗GPT의 주간 9억 사용자 중 4분의 1 이상이 한때 비싼 진료실 방문이 필요했던 의료 질문을 할 수 있게 한 생성형 AI의 발전이 이를 견인했다.

grammarElements.text: While activists prod corporations from the outside
grammarElements.label: While 대조 부사절

grammarElements.text: a new wave of disrupters
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is assaulting American health care from within
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 동사구

grammarElements.text: fuelled by soaring insurance premiums ..., plummeting trust ..., and advances in generative AI
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 + 3중 병렬 (원인)

vocabulary.example: Plummeting trust in institutions has driven patients to seek alternatives.

expressions.usage: 변화의 방향과 원천을 대비시킬 때 사용하는 표현 쌍

patterns.pattern: While [force A] prods from the outside, [force B] is assaulting from within
patterns.explanation: 외부와 내부 양쪽에서 동시에 압력이 가해지는 상황을 그리는 패턴
examples: While regulators push from the outside, whistleblowers are exposing problems from within.
While competitors attack from the outside, cultural decay is weakening the company from within.

drillQuestions.question: Venture-capital funding for digital-health startups ______ to $14 billion in 2025.

sentences.notes: from the outside / from within 대비가 기업 변화의 이중 압력을 한 문장에 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: While activists prod corporations from the outside
koreanAlignment.ko: 행동주의 투자자들이 외부에서 기업을 밀어붙이는 동안

koreanAlignment.en: a new wave of disrupters is assaulting American health care from within
koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 파괴자들의 물결이 내부에서 미국 의료를 공격하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: soaring insurance premiums
plummeting trust in doctors

sentences.original: The health-care insurgency illustrates a broader corporate truth: that industries which resist disruption for years -- whether through regulatory capture, complexity, or sheer opacity -- often face the most violent transformation once the dam breaks, as patients who once deferred to a paternalistic medical hierarchy now demand the same consumer agency they exercise in every other sphere of their lives.
sentences.translation: 의료계의 반란은 더 넓은 기업적 진실을 예증한다: 규제 포획이든, 복잡성이든, 순전한 불투명성이든 간에 수년간 파괴에 저항하는 산업일수록 둑이 무너지면 가장 격렬한 변환에 직면하는 경우가 많다는 것이다. 한때 가부장적 의료 위계에 순응하던 환자들이 이제 삶의 다른 모든 영역에서 행사하는 것과 같은 소비자 주체성을 요구하고 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The health-care insurgency
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: illustrates
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: a broader corporate truth
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: that industries which resist disruption ... often face the most violent transformation
grammarElements.label: 동격 that절 (truth의 내용)

grammarElements.text: as patients who once deferred ... now demand
grammarElements.label: as 이유절

vocabulary.example: A consumer insurgency is reshaping how health care is delivered.

expressions.usage: 오래 억눌려 있던 변화가 갑자기 폭발적으로 일어남을 표현하는 비유

patterns.pattern: [Case] illustrates a broader truth: that [general principle]
patterns.explanation: 구체적 사례에서 일반적 원칙을 도출하는 논증 패턴
examples: The taxi industry illustrates a broader truth: that regulation designed to protect consumers can also shield incumbents from competition.
The banking crisis illustrates a broader truth: that complexity without transparency invites disaster.

drillQuestions.question: Industries which resist disruption often face the most violent transformation once the dam ______.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤의 that절이 핵심 논점을 전달하며, once the dam breaks 비유가 변화의 폭발성을 생생히 그린다.

koreanAlignment.en: The health-care insurgency illustrates a broader corporate truth
koreanAlignment.ko: 의료계의 반란은 더 넓은 기업적 진실을 예증한다

koreanAlignment.en: once the dam breaks
a paternalistic medical hierarchy
the same consumer agency

sentences.original: At the opposite end of the corporate spectrum from high-tech disruption sits the humble question of grunt work, which -- contrary to the breathless promises of AI evangelists -- serves functions far more valuable than its tedium suggests, from filling the days of junior employees who are learning the basics of office life to providing the cognitive downtime that, according to research from the University of Texas, allows creative ideas to incubate and minds to wander productively.
sentences.translation: 하이테크 파괴의 반대편 끝에는 단순 노동이라는 소박한 문제가 자리하고 있는데, 이는 AI 전도사들의 흥분된 약속과 달리 지루함이 시사하는 것보다 훨씬 더 가치 있는 기능을 수행한다 — 사회생활의 기본을 배우는 주니어 직원의 하루를 채우는 것에서부터, 텍사스 대학교 연구에 따르면 창의적 아이디어가 무르익고 정신이 생산적으로 방황할 수 있게 해 주는 인지적 휴식 시간을 제공하는 것에 이르기까지.

grammarElements.text: At the opposite end of the corporate spectrum
grammarElements.label: 장소 부사구 (도치)

grammarElements.text: sits
grammarElements.label: 도치 동사

grammarElements.text: the humble question of grunt work
grammarElements.label: 주어 (도치)

grammarElements.text: which serves functions far more valuable than its tedium suggests
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: Even seemingly pointless drudgery teaches patience and attention to detail.

expressions.phrase: contrary to the breathless promises of
expressions.usage: 과장된 주장에 반론을 제기할 때 쓰는 격식 표현

patterns.pattern: At the opposite end of [spectrum] from [extreme A] sits [overlooked B]
patterns.explanation: 주제를 스펙트럼의 반대편으로 전환하여 간과된 요소를 부각하는 패턴
examples: At the opposite end of the investment spectrum from venture capital sits the humble savings account.
At the opposite end of the media landscape from streaming giants sits the local newspaper.

drillQuestions.question: Grunt work serves functions far more valuable than its ______ suggests.

sentences.notes: 도치 구문(At ... sits)과 contrary to 삽입절이 단순 노동의 숨겨진 가치를 역설적으로 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: At the opposite end of the corporate spectrum
the humble question of grunt work
contrary to the breathless promises of AI evangelists
allows creative ideas to incubate and minds to wander productively
koreanAlignment.ko: 창의적 아이디어가 무르익고 정신이 생산적으로 방황할 수 있게 해 준다

sentences.original: BASF, the German chemicals giant that lost its crown as the world's largest by revenue after Russia's invasion of Ukraine severed its access to cheap gas, exemplifies how even the most disciplined corporate turnaround -- involving 4 billion euros in cost cuts, the sale of a coatings division, and plans to list an agricultural unit -- can be thrown into uncertainty by geopolitical shocks, as the Iran war now threatens to destroy demand and trap a quarter of the world's ethylene and ammonia supply behind the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.translation: 러시아의 우크라이나 침공이 값싼 가스 공급을 끊으면서 매출 기준 세계 최대라는 왕관을 잃은 독일의 화학 대기업 BASF는, 40억 유로의 비용 절감, 도료 사업부 매각, 농업 부문 상장 계획까지 포함한 가장 체계적인 기업 턴어라운드조차도 지정학적 충격으로 불확실성에 빠질 수 있음을 잘 보여 준다 — 이란 전쟁이 이제 수요를 파괴하고 세계 에틸렌·암모니아 공급의 4분의 1을 호르무즈 해협 너머에 가둘 위험을 가하고 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: BASF, the German chemicals giant that lost its crown
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: exemplifies
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: how even the most disciplined corporate turnaround ... can be thrown into uncertainty
grammarElements.label: how 명사절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: as the Iran war now threatens to destroy demand
grammarElements.label: as 이유절

vocabulary.example: The conflict severed supply lines that had been reliable for decades.

expressions.usage: 예상치 못한 충격이 계획을 무력화했을 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Company] exemplifies how even the most [positive effort] can be [undermined] by [external force]
patterns.explanation: 내부 노력이 외부 충격에 의해 좌절될 수 있음을 사례로 보여주는 패턴
examples: Nokia exemplifies how even the most dominant market leader can be blindsided by a shift in technology.
Argentina exemplifies how even the most ambitious reform can be derailed by a currency crisis.

drillQuestions.question: Even the most disciplined corporate turnaround can be thrown into uncertainty by geopolitical ______.

sentences.notes: exemplifies how 구조가 BASF 사례를 통해 지정학이 기업 전략을 압도할 수 있음을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: the German chemicals giant that lost its crown
even the most disciplined corporate turnaround
can be thrown into uncertainty by geopolitical shocks
koreanAlignment.ko: 지정학적 충격으로 불확실성에 빠질 수 있다

sentences.original: The ritual of the annual employee survey, a staple of modern management that companies deploy to gauge worker sentiment, conceals its own paradox: organisations that genuinely care about what their employees think often have the least need for formal surveys, while those that rely on them most heavily tend to be the places where workers, confronted with a Likert scale and the proposition that their bosses communicate like bananas, prudently choose "Neither Agree Nor Disagree."
sentences.translation: 직원 감정을 측정하기 위해 기업이 실시하는 현대 경영의 필수 요소인 연례 직원 설문 의식은 그 자체로 역설을 숨기고 있다: 직원의 생각을 진심으로 걱정하는 조직일수록 공식 설문의 필요성이 가장 적고, 설문에 가장 크게 의존하는 곳일수록 직원들이 리커트 척도와 '상사가 바나나처럼 소통한다'는 명제 앞에서 신중하게 '동의하지도 반대하지도 않음'을 선택하는 곳인 경향이 있다.

grammarElements.text: The ritual of the annual employee survey
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: a staple of modern management that companies deploy to gauge worker sentiment
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명구 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: conceals its own paradox
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: organisations that genuinely care ... while those that rely on them most heavily ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 대조 설명절

vocabulary.example: Managers use surveys to gauge employee morale.

expressions.usage: 겉보기에 합리적인 관행의 내적 모순을 드러낼 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Practice] conceals its own paradox: [those who need it least] ..., while [those who need it most] ...
patterns.explanation: 하나의 관행이 작동해야 할 곳에서 오히려 작동하지 않는 역설을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The annual performance review conceals its own paradox: the best managers rarely need them, while the worst rely on them to avoid difficult conversations.
Data transparency conceals its own paradox: companies with nothing to hide share freely, while those with the most to explain produce the thickest reports.

drillQuestions.question: The ritual of the annual employee survey conceals its own ______.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤 while 대조 구조와 유머러스한 '바나나' 비유가 기업 설문의 허구성을 날카롭게 풍자한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The ritual of the annual employee survey
conceals its own paradox
organisations that genuinely care
prudently choose "Neither Agree Nor Disagree"

sentences.original: Artificial intelligence is reshaping not just how companies compete but how they understand themselves, as algorithms now parse unstructured employee reviews on sites like GlassDoor to identify high-pressure cultures predictive of future corporate scandals -- a development that, combined with the AI-driven disruption of industries from health care to chemicals, suggests that the technology's deepest impact on corporate life may lie less in automating tasks than in making the previously invisible dynamics of organisations legible for the first time.
sentences.translation: 인공지능은 기업이 어떻게 경쟁하는지뿐만 아니라 어떻게 자기 자신을 이해하는지도 재편하고 있다. 알고리즘이 이제 글래스도어 같은 사이트의 비정형 직원 리뷰를 분석하여 미래 기업 스캔들을 예측하는 고압적 문화를 식별하기 때문이다 — 이 발전은 의료에서 화학에 이르는 산업의 AI 주도 파괴와 결합하여, 기술이 기업 생활에 미치는 가장 깊은 영향이 업무 자동화보다는 조직의 이전에 보이지 않던 역학을 처음으로 읽을 수 있게 만드는 데 있을 수 있음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: Artificial intelligence
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is reshaping not just how companies compete but how they understand themselves
grammarElements.label: 동사 + not just A but B 목적어

grammarElements.text: as algorithms now parse unstructured employee reviews
grammarElements.label: as 이유절

grammarElements.text: suggests that the technology's deepest impact ... may lie less in automating tasks than in making ... dynamics legible
grammarElements.label: less A than B 비교절

vocabulary.example: AI makes the hidden patterns of corporate culture legible for the first time.

expressions.phrase: may lie less in A than in B
expressions.usage: 기대되는 영향보다 더 깊은 차원의 영향을 제시할 때 사용하는 비교 구문

patterns.pattern: [Technology] is reshaping not just [expected area] but [unexpected area]
patterns.explanation: 기술의 영향이 예상 범위를 넘어 더 깊은 영역까지 미치고 있음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Social media is reshaping not just how we communicate but how we form identities.
Genomics is reshaping not just how we treat disease but how we understand human potential.

drillQuestions.question: The technology's deepest impact may lie less in automating tasks than in making dynamics ______.

sentences.notes: not just A but B와 less A than B가 이중으로 작동하여 AI의 진짜 영향력이 자동화가 아닌 '가시화'에 있음을 논증한다.

koreanAlignment.en: reshaping not just how companies compete but how they understand themselves
koreanAlignment.ko: 기업이 어떻게 경쟁하는지뿐만 아니라 어떻게 자기 자신을 이해하는지도 재편하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: high-pressure cultures predictive of future corporate scandals
koreanAlignment.ko: 미래 기업 스캔들을 예측하는 고압적 문화

koreanAlignment.en: making the previously invisible dynamics of organisations legible for the first time
koreanAlignment.ko: 조직의 이전에 보이지 않던 역학을 처음으로 읽을 수 있게 만드는 것

sentences.original: What unites these disparate threads -- the activist fund demanding simplification, the startup promising patients agency, the researcher defending drudgery, the chemical executive navigating war, the survey designer wrestling with honesty -- is the recognition that corporate life is governed not by timeless laws but by norms that shift with technology, geopolitics, and the evolving expectations of the people who work within them, a reality that makes adaptability, rather than any single strategy, the most valuable corporate asset of all.
sentences.translation: 이 서로 다른 갈래들을 — 단순화를 요구하는 행동주의 펀드, 환자에게 주체성을 약속하는 스타트업, 잡무를 수호하는 연구자, 전쟁을 헤쳐나가는 화학 기업 임원, 정직과 씨름하는 설문 설계자를 — 하나로 묶는 것은, 기업 생활이 영원한 법칙이 아니라 기술, 지정학, 그리고 그 안에서 일하는 사람들의 진화하는 기대에 따라 변화하는 규범에 의해 지배된다는 인식이며, 이 현실이 어떤 단일 전략이 아니라 적응력을 가장 가치 있는 기업 자산으로 만든다.

grammarElements.text: What unites these disparate threads
grammarElements.label: What 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: be동사

grammarElements.text: the recognition that corporate life is governed not by timeless laws but by norms
grammarElements.label: 보어 + that 동격절

grammarElements.text: a reality that makes adaptability ... the most valuable corporate asset
grammarElements.label: 동격 설명절 + make O C 구조

vocabulary.example: The essay weaves disparate themes into a single coherent argument.
In turbulent times, adaptability matters more than any fixed plan.

expressions.phrase: not by timeless laws but by norms that shift
expressions.meaning: 영원한 법칙이 아니라 변화하는 규범에 의해
expressions.usage: 고정된 원칙 대신 유동적 현실을 강조할 때 쓰는 대조 표현

patterns.pattern: What unites [disparate elements] is the recognition that [unifying insight]
patterns.explanation: 다양한 주제를 하나의 통찰로 통합하는 결론 패턴
examples: What unites these case studies is the recognition that culture eats strategy for breakfast.
What unites these crises is the recognition that fragility compounds in interconnected systems.

drillQuestions.question: Adaptability, rather than any single strategy, is the most valuable corporate ______ of all.

sentences.notes: What unites 구조가 다섯 가지 주제를 하나의 결론으로 묶으며, 적응력이라는 핵심 메시지를 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What unites these disparate threads
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 서로 다른 갈래들을 하나로 묶는 것

koreanAlignment.en: not by timeless laws but by norms that shift
koreanAlignment.ko: 영원한 법칙이 아니라 변화하는 규범에 의해

koreanAlignment.en: adaptability, rather than any single strategy
the most valuable corporate asset of all
